data.narrative:
Frankenburg Technologies is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company that has raised €40M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 10 firms across public and private markets: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and NODA AI, alongside other frontier-defense entrants.

The peer-median method serves as the primary triangulation approach, anchored on 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies. These peers span a valuation range of $125M to $500M, with Stark Defence at the high end ($500M), Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, and NODA AI at $125M. The median across this set is $225M, which represents the central implied valuation for Frankenburg Technologies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M (low) to $259M (high), with a median point of $225M. This range reflects approximately 15% variance bands around the peer median. Key uncertainties include the limited depth of the triangulation methodology—reliance on a single method rather than multiple approaches—and execution risk inherent to early-stage defense-tech companies. Additionally, heterogeneity within the peer set, driven by differences in product maturity and customer traction, introduces variance that single-point estimates do not fully capture.
